Mountains are large landforms that rise prominently above their surroundings and are typically formed through tectonic activity or volcanic processes. Hiking and mountaineering attract millions of outdoor enthusiasts each year, drawn by the challenge of elevation and the reward of sweeping panoramic views. Alpine environments support unique flora and fauna adapted to cold temperatures, thin air, and rocky terrain. The world's tallest mountain, Everest, reaches 8,849 meters above sea level in the Himalayas.
